label: config-management
name: Config Management + Cloud Deployment (OpenTofu monorepo)
summary: Multi-service, multi-env, multi-cloud OpenTofu monorepo: one feature-flagged service module (create/reuse/disabled per primitive), platform/load-balancer/service/registry root layers composed via remote state, hierarchical state keys + var-file envs, and a registry-manifest config-discovery convention.

trigger_phrases: a global way of using open tofu to manage cloud deployments
set up our infrastructure as code
how do we deploy services to the cloud consistently

when_to_use: Deploying multiple containerized services to a cloud with one consistent IaC pattern.
Needing multi-environment (dev/prod) deploys with isolated state and create-or-reuse flexibility per resource.
when_not: A single static site or a lone serverless function with no shared infra.
A team standardized on a different IaC tool (Pulumi/CDK/Helm).
A one-off throwaway environment where the monorepo overhead is not earned.

parameters.name: service_name
parameters.description: the first service to onboard, kebab-case

parameters.name: state_region
parameters.description: region of the state bucket + lock table
